interview_transcript.content: Good evening, ma'am. I'm Officer Johnson with the Oakdale Police Department. I'm here because we received a call about a disturbance at this address. Before we begin, I want you to know that you are safe and I am here to help you. Can you please state your full name and date of birth for the record?

(nervously) Oh, thank you, Officer... Johnson. My name is Leila Morales-Ramirez, and my date of birth is... (pausing for a moment) November 12, 1985. (looking around nervously) I'm just glad you're here. It's been a really scary night.

Thank you, Ms. Morales-Ramirez. I can see you're upset and I want to help. Can you tell me what happened tonight? Please start from the beginning — what time did the incident occur and who was involved?

(taking a deep breath, trying to compose herself) It happened around 9:30 PM, I think. My boyfriend, Rohan Jensen, and I were arguing. It started as a normal fight, but it escalated really quickly. He... (pausing, getting choked up) he got really angry and started yelling at me. Then, he... (shuddering) he grabbed me and started hurting me. I was trying to defend myself, but he's a lot stronger than me. (looking down, tears welling up in her eyes) He had a knife, and I was really scared. I thought he was going to... (trailing off, unable to finish the sentence)

(composing herself slightly) Our neighbor must have heard the noise and called the police, because I heard the sirens outside around 9:50 PM. That's when Rohan stopped and tried to leave, but the police arrived and... (exhaling) and they took him away. (looking up at the officer, pleadingly) I'm just glad they got here when they did. I don't know what would have happened if they hadn't.

You mentioned he grabbed you and started hurting you, and that he had a knife. Can you describe exactly what he did physically — where did he grab you, did he strike you, and what are your injuries? Also, can you describe the knife — what did it look like and where did he get it from?

(wincing, remembering the pain) He... he grabbed me by the neck and arms. He was squeezing my neck really hard, and I thought I was going to pass out. He also hit me in the face, I think with his fist. (touching her face, wincing) My cheek and eye are really sore. And... (pausing, taking a deep breath) he used the knife to threaten me. He didn't stab me, but he held it up to my throat and said he would kill me if I didn't do what he wanted.

(shuddering at the memory) The knife was a big kitchen knife, with a sharp point. I think he got it from the kitchen drawer. I remember seeing him open the drawer and take it out. He was waving it around, saying he would hurt me if I didn't calm down. (looking down, tears streaming down her face) I was so scared. I thought he was going to kill me.

(sniffling, trying to compose herself) My neck is really sore, and I have bruises on my arms and face. (touching her neck, gently) I think he might have tried to strangle me, because my throat is really sore too. (looking up at the officer, pleadingly) I'm just glad he didn't hurt me worse. I don't know what would have happened if the police hadn't arrived when they did.

I'm very concerned about what you've described, and I want to make sure you receive medical attention. First, has Rohan ever physically hurt you before tonight? And do you know where the knife is right now? Is it still in the apartment?

(nodding, tears still streaming down her face) Yes, Rohan has hurt me before. This isn't the first time. He's been getting more and more violent over the past few months. I've tried to leave him, but he always apologizes and promises to change. But he never does. (shaking her head) I'm so scared of him.

And... (thinking for a moment) I think the police took the knife away. I remember seeing one of the officers pick it up and put it in a bag. I don't think it's still in the apartment. (looking around, nervously) I hope it's not. I don't want to see it again.

(sniffling, remembering something) Oh, and there's something else. Rohan has a gun. He keeps it at his apartment, and he's threatened to use it against me before. (looking up at the officer, fear in her eyes) I'm really scared that he might try to hurt me again if he gets out. Can you... can you please make sure he doesn't have access to any guns?

Thank you for telling me about the gun - that information is very important and we will address it. Can you tell me: how long have you and Rohan been in a relationship, and do you live together here at this apartment? Also, you mentioned a neighbor called — do you know which neighbor that was, or who may have witnessed what happened?

(thinking for a moment) Rohan and I have been together for about three years. We don't live together, though. I live here with my two kids, and Rohan has his own apartment. But he's been staying here with me a lot lately, and that's when the fights usually happen.

And... (nodding) I think the neighbor who called the police is Mrs. Patel from next door. She's a really nice lady, and she's always looking out for me and the kids. I've talked to her before about Rohan's behavior, and she's always been really supportive. I'm pretty sure she's the one who called the police, because I remember hearing her yelling at Rohan to stop and then hearing the sirens outside.

(shaking her head) I don't think anyone else witnessed what happened, though. We were inside the apartment, and the door was closed. But Mrs. Patel might have heard some of the noise. She's always been able to hear when Rohan and I are fighting, and she's always tried to help me get away from him. (looking down, tears in her eyes) I'm just glad she was home tonight and was able to call for help.

You mentioned children were present - how old are your children, and were they in the apartment during the incident? Also, is there an existing restraining order or protective order against Rohan? Have you ever called the police before about his behavior? And do you know Rohan's full date of birth and current home address?

(nodding, worried about her kids) My children are 7 and 9 years old, and they were in the apartment when the incident happened. They were in their room, but they might have heard some of the noise. I'm just glad they didn't see anything. (shuddering at the thought) I don't know what I would do if they had seen Rohan hurting me like that.

And... (nodding) yes, there is a protective order against Rohan. I got it a few weeks ago, after he threatened to hurt me and the kids. I've had to call the police before about his behavior, too. This isn't the first time he's gotten violent with me. (sighing) I've tried to get help before, but it's hard to make him stop.

As for his date of birth and address... (thinking for a moment) Rohan's date of birth is August 25, 1982, and his current home address is 1234 Maple Street, Apartment 1A. (looking up at the officer) I hope that helps. I just want to make sure he can't hurt me or the kids again.

(looking down, tears in her eyes) I'm just so scared of him. I don't know what he's capable of. (sniffling) I just want to be safe.

narrative:
INCIDENT REPORT - DOMESTIC VIOLENCE / CRIMINAL THREATS / ASSAULT WITH A DEADLY WEAPON / PROTECTION ORDER VIOLATION

Reporting Officer: Officer Johnson
Date of Report: Current Date
Incident Location: 1457 Calle Del Sol, Apartment 3B, Oakdale, California
Crime Type: Domestic Violence, Assault with a Deadly Weapon, Criminal Threats, Violation of Protective Order
Case Status: Suspect in Custody

VICTIM INFORMATION:
Name: Leila Morales-Ramirez
Date of Birth: November 12, 1985
Address: 1457 Calle Del Sol, Apartment 3B, Oakdale, California

SUSPECT INFORMATION:
Name: Rohan Jensen
Date of Birth: August 25, 1982
Address: 1234 Maple Street, Apartment 1A, Oakdale, California
Relationship to Victim: Boyfriend (3-year relationship)

WITNESS:
Name: Mrs. Patel
Address: Adjacent unit (next door) to Apartment 3B, 1457 Calle Del Sol

NARRATIVE:

On the date of this incident, at approximately 2150 hours, this officer was dispatched to 1457 Calle Del Sol, Apartment 3B, Oakdale, California, in response to a report of a domestic disturbance. Dispatch reported that a female was heard yelling for help at the location.

Upon arrival, this officer contacted victim Leila Morales-Ramirez (DOB: 11/12/1985) at the above address. The victim appeared visibly distressed, with tears streaming down her face, and displayed observable injuries including redness and bruising to her cheek and eye area and visible redness to her neck.

VICTIM STATEMENT:

Victim Morales-Ramirez stated that at approximately 2130 hours, she and her boyfriend, Rohan Jensen (DOB: 08/25/1982), became engaged in a verbal argument at her residence. The victim reported that the argument escalated rapidly, at which point Jensen became physically violent. Specifically, the victim stated that Jensen grabbed her by the neck and arms, applying significant pressure to her neck to the point where she believed she was going to lose consciousness. The victim further stated that Jensen struck her in the face with a closed fist, causing pain and injury to her cheek and eye.

The victim stated that Jensen then retrieved a large kitchen knife from the kitchen drawer and held the knife to her throat. Jensen allegedly stated words to the effect that he would kill her if she did not comply with his demands. The victim stated she was in fear for her life during this encounter.

The victim identified the knife as a large kitchen knife with a sharp point. This officer notes that the knife was collected at the scene by responding officers and placed into evidence.

The victim further stated that at approximately 2150 hours, neighbor Mrs. Patel (next-door unit) apparently heard the disturbance and called police. The victim reported hearing Mrs. Patel yelling at Jensen to stop prior to police arrival. Upon the arrival of law enforcement, Jensen ceased his actions and attempted to leave the premises. Jensen was subsequently detained by responding officers.

MINOR CHILDREN:
The victim disclosed that two minor children, ages 7 and 9, were present in the apartment during the incident. The children were located in their bedroom and did not directly witness the physical assault, although it is believed they may have heard portions of the altercation.

PROTECTIVE ORDER:
The victim reported that an active protective order exists against Jensen, which was obtained approximately two to three weeks prior to this incident following prior threats made against the victim and her children. Jensen's presence at the victim's residence and his conduct this evening constitute a violation of this protective order.

PRIOR INCIDENTS:
The victim disclosed a pattern of escalating domestic violence perpetrated by Jensen over a period of approximately the past several months. The victim reported having previously contacted law enforcement on at least one prior occasion regarding Jensen's violent behavior. The victim stated that she has attempted to end the relationship but that Jensen has repeatedly apologized and promised to change without doing so.

FIREARM / SAFETY CONCERN:
The victim disclosed that Jensen is known to possess a firearm at his residence located at 1234 Maple Street, Apartment 1A. The victim stated that Jensen has previously threatened to use the firearm against her. This officer notes this information as a significant officer and public safety concern warranting follow-up investigation and verification.

INJURIES OBSERVED:
This officer observed the following injuries on the victim: visible redness and bruising to the left cheek and eye area; redness and tenderness to the neck consistent with the reported strangulation attempt. The victim reported soreness to her arms consistent with being forcibly grabbed. The victim was advised to seek medical attention.

EVIDENCE:
One (1) large kitchen knife recovered from the scene by responding officers and placed into an evidence bag.

CHARGES / RECOMMENDED CHARGES:
Based on the evidence gathered and the victim's statement, the following charges are recommended against Rohan Jensen: (1) Domestic Battery; (2) Assault with a Deadly Weapon (knife); (3) Criminal Threats; (4) Strangulation / Attempted Strangulation; (5) Violation of Protective/Restraining Order.

This report is submitted for review and further action.
